factors.summary: Demand is decelerating due to a more challenging economic environment.

evidence.text: LTL tonnage per day decreased 6.9% and 11.0% for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively, primarily due to decreases in LTL shipments per day and LTL weight per shipment for both comparable periods.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Demand is decelerating due to a challenging economic environment, with significant declines in LTL tonnage.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, with revenue per hundredweight increasing.

evidence.text: LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 3.1% and 3.6% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, as compared to the same periods of 2022. Excluding fuel surcharges, LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 8.9% and 8.4% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue per hundredweight has increased, indicating successful price hikes.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments due to increased revenue per hundredweight.

evidence.text: Our LTL revenue per hundredweight includes the impact of lower fuel surcharges resulting from significant declines in the average price of diesel fuel for the comparable periods. Excluding fuel surcharges, LTL revenue per hundredweight increased 8.9% and 8.4% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments with increased revenue per hundredweight.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower LTL volumes.

evidence.text: Revenue decreased $88.4 million, or 5.5%, and $397.8 million, or 8.3%, in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively, as compared to the same periods of 2022. These declines reflect decreases in LTL tons that were partially offset by increases in LTL revenue per hundredweight.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is decelerating due to lower LTL volumes despite pricing increases.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are declining, with LTL shipments and weight per shipment decreasing.

evidence.text: LTL shipments (in thousands) decreased 4.4% and 8.6% for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively. LTL weight per shipment (lbs.) decreased 4.1% and 3.2% for the same periods.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are declining, with significant decreases in LTL shipments and weight per shipment.

factors.summary: Input costs, particularly diesel fuel, are declining, positively impacting margins.

evidence.text: The cost of diesel fuel, excluding fuel taxes, represents the largest component of operating supplies and expenses, and can vary based on both average price per gallon and consumption. Our average cost per gallon of diesel fuel decreased 17.0% and 19.5% in the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively, as compared to the same periods last year.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Declining diesel fuel costs are positively impacting margins significantly.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with a decrease in the average number of active full-time employees.

evidence.text: Our average number of active full-time employees decreased 9.4% and 8.2% for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively, as we reduced our workforce to align with the lower shipping trends and reduction in our volumes.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are manageable with a reduction in the workforce, aligning with lower shipping trends.

factors.summary: Operating margins are compressing due to higher overhead costs despite improvements in some direct costs.

evidence.text: Despite these efforts, the improvements in some of our direct costs as a percentage of revenue were not enough to fully offset the negative impact on our operating ratio of increases in our overhead costs. As a result, our operating ratio increased to 70.6% and 72.1%, respectively, for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Operating margins are compressing due to higher overhead costs despite some cost improvements.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings through workforce reduction and efficiency improvements.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages and benefits decreased $102.3 million, or 5.0%, for the first nine months of 2023 as compared to the same period of 2022, due to a $96.3 million decrease in salaries and wages and a $6.0 million decrease in employee benefit costs.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: The company is successfully reducing costs through workforce reduction and efficiency improvements, leading to significant savings.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving, with increases in platform shipments per hour and P&D stops per hour.

evidence.text: Our platform shipments per hour and P&D stops per hour metrics improved during the third quarter and first nine months of 2023 as compared to the same periods of 2022.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Productivity metrics have shown improvement, indicating efficiency gains which are beneficial for cost margins.

factors.summary: The company is in contraction mode, reducing workforce to align with lower volumes.

evidence.text: Our average number of active full-time employees decreased 9.4% and 8.2% for the third quarter and first nine months of 2023, respectively, as we reduced our workforce to align with the lower shipping trends and reduction in our volumes.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The company is reducing its workforce to align with lower volumes, which is a strategic adjustment without clear positive or negative implications beyond cost management.
